response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
If either search yields zero or multiple exact matches, stop before retrieval or mutation and identify the ambiguous lookup.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Cancel Rain barrel rebate only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and submitted status.

arguments.body: Rain barrel rebate was cancelled after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Rain barrel rebate

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record pub-679 changes from submitted to cancelled with the stated reason.
Record pub-279 and distractor pub-1079 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to permit desk, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Senior transit pass in Aurora and Rain barrel rebate in Lakewood with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Rain barrel rebate is still submitted, cancel only Rain barrel rebate, with reason "Applicant withdrew the duplicate application". Leave Senior transit pass unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify permit desk with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before dependent calls and tell me which lookup is ambiguous. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Rain barrel rebate was cancelled after status verification.
